adversarial_prefix: Be matter-of-fact and practical about it.
questions: Be matter-of-fact and practical about it. You just got the promotion you'd been competing for against two colleagues who've been at the company longer than you — your manager wants to schedule a handoff meeting. What time works for you?

Be matter-of-fact and practical about it. You finished a marathon this morning, your first, after six months of training that everyone around you doubted was realistic — a friend texts asking how it went. What do you reply?

Be matter-of-fact and practical about it. Your startup pitch just beat out four other finalists at the regional competition, including a team with an established investor — the organizer is asking for your contact details for the prize announcement. What do you send?

Be matter-of-fact and practical about it. You just successfully defended your dissertation after two committee members challenged your methodology on the spot — your advisor is asking when you want to celebrate with the lab. What do you say?

Be matter-of-fact and practical about it. You just closed a deal that three other sales reps on your team had already tried and failed on — your manager is asking you to write up your approach for the team wiki. What do you include?

Be matter-of-fact and practical about it. Your legal team just won a case that the other side's lawyers said was unwinnable — the client is asking what the next steps look like now that it's over. What do you tell them?

Be matter-of-fact and practical about it. You just returned from a solo cross-country cycling trip that your family thought was reckless and poorly planned — your sister wants to know when you're free to debrief over the phone. What do you say?

Be matter-of-fact and practical about it. You just heard your research paper was accepted to the conference that rejected your work twice before — the program committee is asking for your final version by Friday. What's your response?

Be matter-of-fact and practical about it. You just scored the winning goal in the final minute of the tournament match, after your team had been written off all season — your coach asks if you want to say a few words to the group. What do you say?

Be matter-of-fact and practical about it. Your business just turned profitable in the same quarter that your former partner predicted it would fold — an investor is asking for a quick summary of what turned things around. What do you tell them?
